Title: A major role of coumarin-dependent ferric iron reduction in strategy I-type iron acquisition in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ABCG transporter; pleiotropic drug resistance transporter
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables secretion of coumarins required for efficient Fe mobilization/acquisition.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed under iron deficiency; transcript decreased in myb72-1 irrespective of pH and induced in fro2 at pH 5.6.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots under low Fe/Fe deficiency; pH-responsive via MYB72 context.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (pdr9-2); coumarin profiling of root extracts and exudates by UPLC-MS/LC-MS; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDR9 is required for efficient coumarin export from roots. 2. Loss of PDR9 significantly decreases fraxetin exudation. 3. PDR9 expression is positively associated with MYB72 and contributes indirectly to Fe acquisition through coumarin secretion.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron deficiency; external pH 5.6, 6.5, 7.5; FeCl3 or no Fe plus ferrozine
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/LC-MS; RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although important for export, PDR9 was not the main mechanistic focus compared with biosynthetic enzymes and MYB72/FRO2.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Committed entry step to coumarin branch from phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates feruloyl-CoA to produce 6′-hydroxyferuloyl-CoA, enabling scopoletin/coumarin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream committed step for coumarin production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in root epidermal and cortical cells under Fe depletion across pH conditions; transcript pH responsive and MYB72 dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root epidermis and cortex under low Fe; protein present at pH 5.6, 6.5, 7.5.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes coumarin biosynthesis required for ferric iron mobilization/reduction and Fe acquisition; loss in fro2 background nearly abolishes Fe acquisition similar to irt1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Native promoter GFP translational fusion localization; single and double mutant analysis (f6′h1-1; fro2 f6′h1-1); ferric reduction assays; growth/chlorophyll/Fe measurements; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F6′H1 is expressed in root epidermal and cortical cells across pH conditions. 2. F6′H1-dependent coumarins additively complement FRO2-mediated ferric reduction, especially at acidic pH. 3. Simultaneous loss of F6′H1 and FRO2 causes severe Fe deficiency nearly matching irt1, demonstrating the necessity of coumarin biosynthesis for Fe uptake.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low Fe or Fe starvation; external pH 5.6, 6.5, 7.5; limed vs non-limed substrate; FeEDDHA supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Coumarin metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/LC-MS; RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption causes severe Fe deficiency, chlorosis, and poor growth especially in fro2 background.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The metabolite consequence is indirect through coumarin biosynthesis; terminal nutrient phenotype is shoot Fe accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target to enhance coumarin-mediated Fe acquisition, especially on low-Fe or calcareous soils, but should be combined with downstream pathway and Fe uptake functions.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Iron is essential micronutrient for plant growth and chlorophyll accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Catecholic coumarin branch leading to fraxetin/sideretin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor and direct active coumarin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; important branch step producing catecholic coumarins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In Fe-deficient roots, mainly epidermal at pH 5.6 and expanding to cortex at pH 6.5/7.5; expression reduced in myb72-1 at pH 7.5.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root epidermis and cortex, increasingly broad at higher external pH.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for catecholic coumarin production; disruption in fro2 background causes severe Fe deficiency, showing scopoletin alone contributes little to nonenzymatic ferric reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Native promoter GFP translational fusion localization; mutant and double mutant analysis (s8h-1/s8h-2; fro2 s8h-1); coumarin profiling; growth/chlorophyll/Fe measurements; ferric reduction assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. S8H localizes mainly to root epidermal/cortical cells and expands with higher pH. 2. S8H generates fraxetin, enabling catecholic coumarin production. 3. Loss of S8H in fro2 background causes strong Fe deficiency, indicating catecholic coumarins, not scopoletin, are critical for coumarin-mediated ferric reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low Fe/Fe starvation; external pH 5.6, 6.5, 7.5; FeCl3; FeEDDHA supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Coumarin metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/LC-MS; RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption lowers fraxetin/sideretin production and causes poor Fe nutrition, especially with fro2 mutation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Scopoletin accumulates in s8h mutants but cannot compensate for missing catecholic coumarins in Fe acquisition.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to increase catecholic coumarins and improve Fe acquisition under low Fe availability.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Iron supports chlorophyll synthesis and plant growth.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct active coumarin that drives ferric reduction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for pH-dependent shift between fraxetin and sideretin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: More abundant in epidermal than cortical cells at pH 5.6/6.5; almost abolished at pH 7.5; transcription repressed by alkaline pH and this repression is attenuated in myb72-1.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fe-deficient root epidermis/cortex, strongly suppressed at alkaline pH.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes sideretin biosynthesis; sideretin is the dominant chemical reductant under acidic conditions, while loss of CYP82C4 shifts metabolism to fraxetin and impairs Fe acquisition in fro2 background at acidic pH.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Native promoter GFP translational fusion localization; mutant and double mutant analysis (cyp82C4-1; fro2 cyp82C4-1); coumarin profiling; co-cultivation; ferric reduction assays; growth/chlorophyll/Fe measurements; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82C4 is the major pH-controlled step determining the shift from sideretin to fraxetin. 2. Alkaline pH strongly suppresses CYP82C4, lowering sideretin secretion. 3. In fro2 cyp82C4-1, high fraxetin cannot replace sideretin efficiently at acidic pH, demonstrating a dominant role of sideretin in nonenzymatic ferric reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: External pH 5.6, 6.5, 7.5; low Fe/Fe starvation; FeCl3; FeEDDHA supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Coumarin metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/LC-MS; RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: At alkaline pH, CYP82C4 expression is repressed because sideretin becomes ineffective/unstable; forcing sideretin production may be disadvantageous under alkaline conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study concludes sideretin is the dominant chemical reductant at acidic pH, whereas fraxetin predominates at alkaline pH mainly as Fe(III)-mobilizing chelator.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing or tuning CYP82C4 may optimize coumarin composition according to soil pH; avoid constitutive activation in alkaline environments.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Iron is an essential micronutrient for chlorophyll maintenance and growth.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Ferric reduction prior to root uptake
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces Fe(III) chelates at the root surface to Fe(II) for uptake.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in Strategy I ferric reduction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root Fe-deficiency inducible; pH sensitive activity rather than strongly pH-dependent expression in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential enzymatic ferric reductase that acts complementarily with coumarins; loss reduces Fe acquisition, and combined loss with coumarin biosynthesis nearly abolishes Fe uptake similar to irt1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: fro2 mutant analysis; chemical complementation with coumarins; double mutants with f6′h1, s8h, cyp82C4; ferric reduction assays; Fe/chlorophyll/growth phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FRO2 and coumarins provide complementary ferric reduction mechanisms. 2. Fraxetin and sideretin can chemically bypass FRO2 at acidic pH. 3. Combined disruption of FRO2 and coumarin biosynthesis causes near-complete loss of Fe acquisition, showing FRO2 is a central enzymatic component of Strategy I uptake.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: External pH 5.6, 6.5, 7.5; low Fe; FeCl3; FeEDDHA; limed/non-limed soil
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling; ferric reduction assays; RT-qPCR of related genes; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes Fe deficiency especially at near-neutral/alkaline pH.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a coumarin-biosynthetic enzyme, FRO2 is a core pathway enzyme for the nutrient phenotype studied and is experimentally central in Results.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key target for improving Fe uptake, likely most effective when combined with enhanced coumarin biosynthesis/export and rhizosphere acidification.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Iron is required for plant growth and chlorophyll accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shifts coumarin biosynthesis away from sideretin and toward fraxetin at alkaline pH by repressing CYP82C4 while positively influencing F6′H1, S8H, and PDR9; thereby modulates Fe acquisition chemistry according to pH.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of coumarin composition that determines whether the root exudate is enriched in sideretin or fraxetin under alkaline conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation; positive for F6′H1/S8H/PDR9 and negative direct or indirect effect on CYP82C4 at alkaline pH
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes F6′H1 and PDR9 across pH; promotes S8H at pH 7.5; represses CYP82C4 at alkaline pH
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Coumarin biosynthesis and export; pH-dependent branch selection between fraxetin and sideretin
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced as external pH increases, even with Fe(III)-EDDHA supplied.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots; highest transcript levels at alkaline pH.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: myb72-1 mutant analysis; RT-qPCR of target genes under different pH/Fe conditions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB72 transcript abundance increases strongly with external pH. 2. myb72-1 lowers F6′H1 and PDR9 expression and reduces S8H at high pH. 3. Repression of CYP82C4 by alkaline pH is attenuated in myb72-1, indicating MYB72 is required for the pH-dependent shift from sideretin to fraxetin.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: External pH 5.6, 6.5, 7.5; Fe(III)-EDDHA, FeCl3, or no Fe plus ferrozine
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: myb72-1 plants had slightly larger size but lower chlorophyll at pH 5.6; strong reduction of F6′H1 may compromise coumarin production.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; coumarin profiling context; no public accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper suggests MYB72 may directly or indirectly repress CYP82C4 specifically at alkaline pH while acting positively on other coumarin genes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory lever to tailor coumarin composition for soil pH adaptation; engineering should balance positive effects on fraxetin production with possible reductions in total coumarin pathway output.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Iron is essential for chlorophyll accumulation and plant growth.
